name: AIF360 (AI Fairness 360)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); IBM Research / Trusted AI

performance.notes: Fairness metrics are fast; bias mitigation algorithms vary in complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Fairness metrics and bias mitigation for ML. from aif360.datasets import BinaryLabelDataset. from aif360.metrics import BinaryLabelDatasetMetric, ClassificationMetric. dataset = BinaryLabelDataset(df=df, label_names=['label'], protected_attribute_names=['race']). metric = BinaryLabelDatasetMetric(dataset, privileged_groups=[{'race': 1}], unprivileged_groups=[{'race': 0}]). metric.mean_difference(). metric.disparate_impact(). ClassificationMetric: equal_opportunity_difference(), equalized_odds_difference(). Preprocessing: Reweighing(), DisparateImpactRemover(), LFR(). Inprocessing: AdversarialDebiasing(), PrejudiceRemover(). Postprocessing: EqOddsPostprocessing(), RejectOptionClassification().

gotchas: BinaryLabelDataset wraps pandas — careful with data type conversion
Fairness metrics conflict — can't optimize all simultaneously (fairness impossibility theorems)
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Fairness is not just a technical problem — business and legal context required
best_when: Computing fairness metrics: disparate impact, equal opportunity, equalized odds
Bias mitigation in hiring, credit scoring, or criminal justice ML applications
Regulatory compliance requiring demonstrated fairness analysis
avoid_when: Multi-class fairness (limited support beyond binary classification)
When fairlearn has sufficient metrics (lighter dependency)
Fairness in computer vision (use specialized tools)
summary: AIF360 measures and mitigates ML bias. BinaryLabelDataset + ClassificationMetric for disparate impact, equal opportunity, equalized odds. Pre-processing: Reweighing, DisparateImpactRemover. In-processing: AdversarialDebiasing. Post-processing: EqOddsPostprocessing. Apache-2.0 by IBM Research. Best for comprehensive bias auditing pipelines. Some mitigators need TF. No type annotations. Fairness tradeoffs inherent (impossibility theorems).
